name: Addressing DEI Discrimination by Federal Contractors
summary: This Executive Order prevents federal contractors and subcontractors from engaging in racially discriminatory diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) activities by requiring specific contract clauses and establishing enforcement mechanisms to promote economy and efficiency in federal contracting.
affects: Federal contractors and subcontractors, and federal agencies involved in contracting

children.name: Policy and Purpose
children.summary: The Administration aims to end racial discrimination in society, including DEI activities that treat individuals differently based on race or ethnicity, deeming such practices unethical, illegal, inefficient, and costly for federal contracts.
children.affects: Federal Government contracting practices and federal contractors

children.name: Definitions
children.summary: This section defines 'racially discriminatory DEI activities' as disparate treatment based on race or ethnicity in various employment, contracting, or program participation contexts, and 'program participation' to include training, mentoring, and other opportunities.
children.affects: Federal contractors and federal agencies implementing the order

children.name: Racially Discriminatory DEI Activities
children.summary: Defines 'racially discriminatory DEI activities' as treating people differently based on race or ethnicity in areas like recruitment, employment, contracting, or resource allocation.
children.affects: Federal contractors and subcontractors

children.name: Program Participation
children.summary: Defines 'program participation' to include membership or access to training, mentoring, leadership development, educational opportunities, clubs, or associations sponsored by a contractor.
children.affects: Federal contractors and their employees or participants

children.name: Requirements for Federal Contractors
children.summary: Federal agencies must include a specific clause in all contracts and subcontracts within 30 days, obligating contractors not to engage in racially discriminatory DEI activities and to comply with reporting and enforcement.
children.affects: Federal contractors and subcontractors

children.name: Prohibition on Discriminatory DEI Activities
children.summary: Contractors must agree not to engage in racially discriminatory DEI activities as defined by the Executive Order.
children.affects: Federal contractors and subcontractors

children.name: Information and Reporting
children.summary: Contractors must furnish all required information, reports, and access to records to verify compliance with the clause.
children.affects: Federal contractors and subcontractors

children.name: Noncompliance Penalties
children.summary: Contracts may be canceled, terminated, or suspended, and the contractor declared ineligible for future government contracts, due to noncompliance.
children.affects: Federal contractors and subcontractors

children.name: Subcontractor Violations Reporting
children.summary: Contractors must report any known or reasonably knowable subcontractor conduct that violates the clause and take directed remedial actions.
children.affects: Federal contractors managing subcontractors

children.name: Subcontractor Lawsuits Notification
children.summary: Contractors must inform the contracting agency if a subcontractor sues them, challenging the validity of this clause.
children.affects: Federal contractors involved in legal disputes with subcontractors

children.name: Compliance as Material to Payment
children.summary: Compliance with the clause is considered material to government payment decisions, allowing for False Claims Act applicability.
children.affects: Federal contractors subject to government payment decisions and the False Claims Act

children.name: Penalties and Enforcement
children.summary: This section outlines how agencies will enforce the requirements, including guidance from OMB, contract termination, debarment, agency reviews, and potential False Claims Act actions by the Attorney General.
children.affects: Federal agencies, contractors, and subcontractors

children.name: OMB Guidance and Agency Actions
children.summary: The OMB Director will issue guidance to ensure compliance, empowering agencies to cancel contracts and suspend or debar non-compliant contractors and subcontractors.
children.affects: Office of Management and Budget, federal contracting agencies, contractors, and subcontractors

children.name: Identification of Risky Economic Sectors
children.summary: OMB, in coordination with other officials, will identify economic sectors at high risk for discriminatory DEI activities and issue further compliance guidance.
children.affects: Office of Management and Budget, Attorney General, Assistant to the President for Domestic Policy, EEOC, and specific economic sectors

children.name: Agency Head Reviews and Reporting
children.summary: Each agency head must review their implementation of the contractor requirements within 120 days and regularly report to the Assistant to the President for Domestic Policy.
children.affects: Heads of executive departments and agencies

children.name: Attorney General Actions
children.summary: The Attorney General will consider bringing False Claims Act actions against violators and ensure prompt review of private False Claims Act lawsuits related to federal contracts.
children.affects: Attorney General, Department of Justice, federal contractors, and private citizens filing False Claims Act lawsuits

children.name: Regulations and Implementation
children.summary: The Federal Acquisition Regulatory Council will amend the Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) to incorporate the new contract clause and remove conflicting provisions, also issuing interim guidance.
children.affects: Federal Acquisition Regulatory Council, federal agencies, and federal contracting processes

children.name: FAR Amendments
children.summary: The FAR Council will amend the Federal Acquisition Regulation to include the new contract clause and remove any conflicting provisions.
children.affects: Federal Acquisition Regulatory Council and federal contracting regulations

children.name: Interim Guidance for Agencies
children.summary: The FAR Council will issue interim guidance within 60 days to help agencies implement the new clause before the full FAR amendments are completed.
children.affects: Federal Acquisition Regulatory Council and federal agencies

children.name: General Provisions
children.summary: This section includes standard legal clauses ensuring the order's severability, not impairing existing authorities, being subject to appropriations, and not creating private rights enforceable against the U.S. government, with publication costs borne by the Department of Justice.
children.affects: All parties affected by the Executive Order, and the Department of Justice

children.name: Severability
children.summary: If any part of this order is found invalid, the remaining provisions and their application will not be affected.
children.affects: Legal interpretation of the Executive Order

children.name: Non-impairment of Authority
children.summary: Nothing in the order should be interpreted to diminish the lawful authority of executive departments, agencies, or the Director of OMB.
children.affects: Executive departments, agencies, and the Director of OMB

children.name: Implementation Subject to Law and Appropriations
children.summary: The order will be implemented consistent with applicable law and only if funds are available.
children.affects: Federal agencies and the implementation of the Executive Order

children.name: No Private Right of Action
children.summary: This order does not create any new legal rights or benefits that individuals or entities can enforce against the U.S. government.
children.affects: Private citizens or entities seeking to challenge the U.S. government

children.name: Cost of Publication
children.summary: The Department of Justice will cover the costs associated with publishing this Executive Order.
children.affects: Department of Justice budget
